Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Hypericum sampsonii; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Hypericum sampsonii; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UbiA-type aromatic prenyltransferase with unusual hybrid aspartate-rich motifs (PHB-like loop 2 and HG-like loop 6)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Type A PPAP branch; prenylative cyclization branch of phlorbenzophenone- and phlorisobutyrophenone-derived PPAPs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: grandone; kolanone; colupulone; diprenylated-monogeranylated phlorisobutyrophenone precursor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a dimethylallyl group from DMAPP to prenylated acylphloroglucinol substrates and catalyses regiospecific intramolecular cyclization to form type A bridged bicyclo[3.3.1]nonane PPAP scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms core type A PPAP products and immediate precursors of downstream caged PPAPs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of type A PPAP scaffolds; converts grandone to 7-epi-nemorosone and kolanone to nemosampsone; also forms aliphatic type A hyperforin analogues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves for 7-epi-nemorosone and nemosampsone in H. sampsonii; roots for 7-epi-secohyperforin/aliphatic analogue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher relative expression in roots than HsCPTb by RT-qPCR; present in H. sampsonii tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves enabled product formation after substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL1 inducible promoter in S. cerevisiae; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in N. benthamiana transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast microsomes; in vitro enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; transient expression and feeding in Nicotiana benthamiana leaf disks; LC-MS/MS product identification; mutagenesis; docking/modeling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate feeding with grandone in N. benthamiana leaf disks; in vitro assay pH affected measured activity optimum
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major yield differences reported between HsCPTa and HsCPTb expression in N. benthamiana; broader substrate scope than HsCPTb may divert flux toward multiple type A products
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful gateway enzyme for synthetic biology and metabolic engineering of medicinal PPAPs, especially type A scaffolds and hyperforin analogues; may be stacked with upstream acylphloroglucinol/prenylation genes for sustainable production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Prefers DMAPP over GPP as donor; does not prenylate unsubstituted phlorbenzophenone aromatic core; nine amino-acid substitutions can convert HsCPTa regiospecificity toward HsCPTb-like activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HsCPTa is a bifunctional prenyltransferase/cyclase from H. sampsonii that forms type A PPAP scaffolds from a common precursor. 2. It converts grandone to 7-epi-nemorosone and kolanone to nemosampsone, and also accepts aliphatic substrates to generate 7-epi-secohyperforin-related products. 3. In planta feeding assays in N. benthamiana confirmed its selective type A activity. 4. Structural and mutational analyses showed that specific active-site residues determine its regiospecific cyclization mode.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactivities reported for PPAPs include antiviral, antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory and cytotoxic activities; hyperforin is antidepressant-related

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Hypericum sampsonii; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Hypericum sampsonii; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UbiA-type aromatic prenyltransferase with unusual hybrid aspartate-rich motifs (PHB-like loop 2 and HG-like loop 6)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Type B PPAP branch; prenylative cyclization branch of phlorbenzophenone-derived PPAPs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a dimethylallyl group from DMAPP to grandone and catalyses regiospecific intramolecular cyclization to form the type B bridged bicyclo[3.3.1]nonane PPAP scaffold
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms core type B PPAP product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of the type B PPAP scaffold by converting grandone to 7-epi-clusianone; does not accept kolanone or aliphatic substrates tested
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Lower relative expression than HsCPTa in roots; associated with tissues producing phlorbenzophenone-derived PPAPs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves enabled selective product formation after substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL1 inducible promoter in S. cerevisiae; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in N. benthamiana transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast microsomes; in vitro enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; transient expression and feeding in Nicotiana benthamiana leaf disks; LC-MS/MS product identification; docking/modeling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate feeding with grandone in N. benthamiana leaf disks; in vitro pH optimum was 10
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Narrow substrate specificity may limit engineering flexibility; no activity with geranyl-containing substrates tested
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for selective biosynthesis of type B PPAP scaffolds in engineered systems and for branch control between type A and type B PPAP products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Does not accept GPP donor; does not prenylate phlorbenzophenone aromatic core; active-site residues support an inverted substrate-binding mode relative to HsCPTa
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HsCPTb is a bifunctional prenyltransferase/cyclase from H. sampsonii that selectively forms the type B PPAP product 7-epi-clusianone from grandone. 2. It differs from HsCPTa in substrate scope and regiospecific cyclization outcome. 3. N. benthamiana feeding assays confirmed its in planta type B activity. 4. Structural comparison with HsCPTa revealed inverted substrate binding as the basis for regiodivergent product formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PPAPs possess medicinal activities including antiviral, antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory and cytotoxic effects

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UbiA-type aromatic prenyltransferase with same unusual metal-ion binding motif variation as HsCPTa
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses DMAPP to prenylate triprenylated or geranylated phlorisobutyrophenone substrates and catalyses cyclization to form hyperforin-related aliphatic PPAPs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms terminal or immediate hyperforin-related products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of hyperforin and secohyperforin from aliphatic acylphloroglucinol precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key scaffold-forming enzyme for hyperforin branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and fruits of H. perforatum
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcripts upregulated in flower tissues based on FPKM values
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL1 inducible promoter in S. cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Database homology search; heterologous expression in yeast microsomes; in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS/MS product profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable enzyme for biotechnological production of hyperforin and related medicinal PPAPs in synthetic biology platforms
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression mining from Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource database; functional assay in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Forms exo secohyperforin, unlike HsCPTa which forms 7-epi-secohyperforin; HsCPTb showed no comparable activity on these aliphatic substrates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HpCPTa1 was identified by sequence homology to HsCPTa and is expressed in H. perforatum tissues associated with hyperforin accumulation. 2. Recombinant HpCPTa1 converts colupulone to secohyperforin and a geranylated precursor to hyperforin. 3. Its product stereochemistry and cyclization mode differ from HsCPTa, explaining species-specific hyperforin-type PPAP biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hyperforin is the antidepressant constituent of St. John's wort
